blurb: App extensions enable third-party functionality within system contexts – keyboards, share sheets, content blockers, widgets – while maintaining strict security isolation. Each extension runs as a separate process from both its containing app and the host app, communicating exclusively via XPC with serialized data. Extensions receive a separate, scoped sandbox and entitlement set, limiting data access compared to a full application. System Extensions on macOS allow vendors to extend kernel functionality – such as network filtering and endpoint security – in user space, eliminating the risk profile of traditional kernel extensions and confining any bugs to a user-space process.
